verses.text: The rich cast gifts into the treasury; a poor widow casts in two mites, 'more than they all,' for she gave all her living. True giving. God weighs the gift by the heart and the cost.
The rich cast gifts into the treasury; a poor widow casts in two mites, 'more than they all,' for she gave all her living. True giving. God weighs the gift by the heart and the cost.

As they admire the temple, 'there shall not be left one stone upon another.' Coming ruin. The proud building will be utterly thrown down.
As they admire the temple, 'there shall not be left one stone upon another.' Coming ruin. The proud building will be utterly thrown down.
As they admire the temple, 'there shall not be left one stone upon another.' Coming ruin. The proud building will be utterly thrown down.
Take heed; many shall come in His name; wars and earthquakes; 'but the end is not by and by.' Beware deception. Such troubles are signs, not yet the end.
Take heed; many shall come in His name; wars and earthquakes; 'but the end is not by and by.' Beware deception. Such troubles are signs, not yet the end.

Before all this they shall persecute you; 'I will give you a mouth and wisdom' none can resist. Promised help. God equips His witnesses to answer their foes.

Ye shall be betrayed and hated, yet 'not an hair of your head' shall perish; 'in your patience possess ye your souls.' Kept and patient. God guards His own through every trial.

When Jerusalem is compassed, flee; great distress shall come, till 'the times of the Gentiles be fulfilled.' Judgment foretold. The doomed city falls in its appointed hour.

Signs in the heavens; 'men's hearts failing them for fear.' Dread day. The end fills the careless world with terror.
Signs in the heavens; 'men's hearts failing them for fear.' Dread day. The end fills the careless world with terror.
They shall see 'the Son of man coming in a cloud'; 'look up... for your redemption draweth nigh.' Glad hope. What terrifies the world is the believer's deliverance.
They shall see 'the Son of man coming in a cloud'; 'look up... for your redemption draweth nigh.' Glad hope. What terrifies the world is the believer's deliverance.
As the fig tree's buds tell of summer, so these signs tell the kingdom is near. Read the times. God gives His people signs to mark the season.
As the fig tree's buds tell of summer, so these signs tell the kingdom is near. Read the times. God gives His people signs to mark the season.
As the fig tree's buds tell of summer, so these signs tell the kingdom is near. Read the times. God gives His people signs to mark the season.
'Heaven and earth shall pass away: but my words shall not pass away.' Sure word. All else perishes, but Christ's word stands for ever.
'Heaven and earth shall pass away: but my words shall not pass away.' Sure word. All else perishes, but Christ's word stands for ever.
Take heed lest that day come unawares; 'watch... and pray always.' Stay ready. The hidden hour calls for constant watchfulness.
Take heed lest that day come unawares; 'watch... and pray always.' Stay ready. The hidden hour calls for constant watchfulness.

By day He taught in the temple, at night on Olivet; the people came early to hear. Tireless teaching. He spends Himself in the work of the gospel.
